title: Run a Pre Bag Out Exchange for the Lowest 25 Percent of Destinations per Sorter Leg
summary: Use the pre bag out function in the bag out control area to exchange the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg before a full bag out. The source defines the pre bag out behavior and references bag out control screens, but does not provide the exact interface click sequence.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to perform a pre bag out action and the intended behavior is to exchange the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as instructions for a full system bag out; the source distinguishes pre bag out from system bag out that closes all destinations and exchanges all totes with parcels in them.
Do not use this runbook when an exact interface sequence or destination-selection display method is required; the source does not define those details.

responsibilities: Access the bag out control area.
Initiate the pre bag out action.
Observe that the action aligns with the documented lowest-25-percent-per-leg behavior.
Confirm the action performed was pre bag out rather than a full bag out.

safety_notes: The source provides operational behavior only and does not specify lockout/tagout, production stop, or other safety isolation requirements.
Do not assume this procedure is the same as a full system bag out; the source explicitly distinguishes the two behaviors.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the bag out control system
Visibility to destination or exchange activity by sorter leg

access_required: Access to the bag out control system
system_preconditions: Bag out control functionality is available to the user in the system interface.
Visibility is available to observe destination or exchange activity by sorter leg.

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame describing overall system bag out control behavior, including pre bag out exchanging the lowest 25% of destinations per sorter leg.
Training frame showing the main overview screen with bag out available on the right-hand control area.

steps.title: Open bag out control and identify pre bag out
steps.purpose: Locate the bag out control area and identify the pre bag out option referenced by the source.
steps.instruction: Access the bag out control system from the overall system interface and identify the pre bag out function within the bag out control area.
steps.expected_result: The operator can see the bag out control area and identify the pre bag out option.
steps.healthy_condition: Bag out control is visible and the pre bag out option is identifiable in the referenced control context.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot locate bag out control or cannot identify a pre bag out option from the available interface.

actions.label: Open bag out control
actions.exact_action: Access the bag out control system and identify the pre bag out function.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Main overview screen showing bag out among the right-hand side controls.
Bag out control reference describing pre bag out and its lowest-25-percent-per-leg behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Bag out control is not accessible.
The pre bag out option cannot be identified from the available interface or source-supported screen references.

source_evidence.text: Access the bag out control system and identify the pre bag out function.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The right-hand side includes startup, shutdown, bag out, purge, resume, and pause all AGVs.
Overall system bag out control behavior includes pre-bag-out.

steps.title: Start pre bag out
steps.purpose: Initiate the pre bag out action from the bag out control area.
steps.instruction: Start the pre bag out action from the bag out control area.
steps.expected_result: The system begins the pre bag out action.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts the pre bag out action and begins exchange activity consistent with pre bag out.
steps.failure_condition: The action does not start, or the resulting behavior appears inconsistent with pre bag out.

actions.label: Start pre bag out
actions.exact_action: Start the pre bag out action.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bag out control reference where pre bag out is described as an available option.

stop_or_escalate_if: The pre bag out action cannot be started.
The system behavior after initiation does not appear to match the documented pre bag out behavior.

source_evidence.text: Start the pre bag out action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

steps.title: Apply the documented pre bag out rule
steps.purpose: Use the source-defined rule to understand and verify what pre bag out is expected to do.
steps.instruction: Use the documented rule that pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands that pre bag out scope is limited to the lowest 25 percent of destinations per sorter leg.
steps.healthy_condition: The expected scope is clearly recognized as lowest 25 percent per leg, not all destinations.
steps.failure_condition: The observed or assumed scope differs from the documented lowest-25-percent-per-leg rule.

actions.label: Verify expected scope
actions.exact_action: Use the documented rule that pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Text stating that pre-bag-out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observed behavior does not align with the documented lowest 25 percent of destinations per sorter leg rule.

source_evidence.text: Use the documented rule that pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.
Pre bag out behavior: exchanges the lowest 25 percent of destinations per sorter leg.

steps.title: Observe exchange activity for included destinations
steps.purpose: Confirm that exchange activity is occurring for destinations included in the pre bag out scope.
steps.instruction: Observe exchange activity for destinations included in the lowest 25 percent on each sorter leg.
steps.expected_result: Exchange activity is observed for destinations that fall within the documented pre bag out scope.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed exchange activity is limited to destinations consistent with the lowest 25 percent per sorter leg.
steps.failure_condition: Exchange activity appears inconsistent with the documented scope, or the system does not provide enough visibility to determine which destinations are included.

actions.label: Observe exchange activity
actions.exact_action: Observe exchange activity for destinations included in that lowest 25 percent per leg.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference slide describing the expected pre bag out scope for comparison with observed behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observed pre bag out behavior does not align with the documented lowest 25 percent per sorter leg rule.
The system does not define or display which destinations are included in the 25 percent, preventing confirmation.

source_evidence.text: Observe exchange activity for destinations included in that lowest 25 percent per leg.
Escalate if the observed pre bag out behavior does not align with the documented lowest 25 percent per sorter leg rule.
The source does not define how the system displays which destinations are included in the 25 percent.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

steps.title: Confirm the action was pre bag out and not full bag out
steps.purpose: Differentiate the completed action from a full system bag out using the source-defined behavior.
steps.instruction: Record or confirm that the action performed was pre bag out rather than a full system bag out.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms the action matched pre bag out behavior and not full system bag out behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: The completed action is recognized as exchanging the lowest 25 percent of destinations per leg rather than closing all destinations and exchanging all totes with parcels.
steps.failure_condition: The action appears to have behaved like a full system bag out, or the operator cannot distinguish the action outcome from full bag out using available evidence.

actions.label: Confirm action type
actions.exact_action: Record or confirm that the action performed was pre bag out rather than a full system bag out.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pre bag out description contrasted with overall system bag out behavior on the same training reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: The action appears to have closed all destinations and exchanged all totes with parcels in them, indicating full system bag out behavior rather than pre bag out.
The operator cannot confirm whether pre bag out or full bag out was performed.

source_evidence.text: Record or confirm that the action performed was pre bag out rather than a full system bag out.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: System bag out closes all destinations and exchanges totes or chutes with parcels in them; pre-bag-out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.

success_criteria: Pre bag out is initiated from the bag out control area.
The resulting behavior matches the documented rule: exchange of the lowest 25 percent of destinations on each sorter leg.
The action is confirmed as pre bag out rather than a full system bag out.
healthy_conditions: Pre bag out is identifiable as a distinct option or function in the bag out control context.
Observed exchange behavior aligns with the documented lowest 25 percent of destinations per sorter leg.
failure_conditions: Pre bag out cannot be located or started.
Observed behavior does not align with the documented lowest 25 percent per sorter leg rule.
The available interface does not make it possible to determine which destinations are included in the 25 percent.
The action appears to behave like a full system bag out instead of pre bag out.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed pre bag out behavior does not align with the documented lowest 25 percent per sorter leg rule.
Escalate if the interface does not provide enough information to determine which destinations are included in the 25 percent.
Escalate if the action appears to perform a full system bag out instead of pre bag out.

Main overview screen showing bag out access point.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Pre bag out exchanges the lowest 25% of destinations per leg of the sorter.
